# Implementation Plan: Core Parse-Extract-Render Pipeline (MVP)

**Branch**: `001-core-pipeline` | **Date**: 2026-03-01 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/001-core-pipeline/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` workflow

## Summary

Implement the foundational three-stage pipeline for Anodyse: YAML Parser → Annotation Extractor → Markdown Renderer. The tool accepts Ansible playbooks or role directories as input and produces user-facing Markdown documentation. MVP includes CLI entry point with directory-first discovery (recursive scan + optional .anodyse.yml manifest), annotation extraction at all YAML hierarchy levels, Jinja2-based rendering with user-overridable templates, and automatic index generation. Exit codes follow constitution: 0 (success), 1 (parse error), 2 (warnings with degraded output).

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Python 3.11+  
**Primary Dependencies**: click (CLI framework), ruamel.yaml (YAML parsing with comment preservation), jinja2 (template rendering)  
**Storage**: File-based I/O only (read YAML, write Markdown) — no database  
**Testing**: pytest with ≥80% coverage requirement  
**Target Platform**: Linux/macOS (Windows support deferred to v2)
**Project Type**: CLI tool for documentation generation  
**Performance Goals**: Generate docs for 20-task playbook in <2 seconds  
**Constraints**: Fully offline (no external API calls), read-only source files, UTF-8 output only  
**Scale/Scope**: MVP handles single playbooks/roles and batch directory processing with recursive discovery

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

### Core Principles Alignment

- [x] **User-First Output**: Spec explicitly requires "Undocumented" notices over hallucinated descriptions (FR-007), task summaries for non-technical users (FR-011), and Mermaid diagrams for visual understanding (US4)
- [x] **Annotation-Driven**: FR-007 mandates None for missing @description; FR-008 warns but continues without annotations
- [x] **Graceful Degradation**: US1 validates skeleton docs work without annotations; US5 scenario 4 validates template incompatibility produces degraded output with exit code 2
- [x] **Fail Loudly in CI, Fail Gently on CLI**: FR-018 defines exit codes 0/1/2; warnings emit but processing continues (exit 2)
- [x] **Convention Over Configuration**: FR-009 zero-config directory scan; optional .anodyse.yml manifest; CLI defaults to ./docs output

### Code Standards Alignment

- [x] **Python 3.11+ only**: Specified in Technical Context
- [x] **Type hints required**: All dataclasses (PlaybookData, RoleData, TaskData) explicitly typed per spec Key Entities section
- [x] **Docstrings required**: Plan requires docstrings on all public functions (Constraints section)
- [x] **PEP 8 + ruff**: Project structure includes ruff configuration
- [x] **pytest tests required**: SC-010 mandates ≥80% coverage
- [x] **No external API calls**: Assumptions section confirms offline-only operation
- [x] **Minimal dependencies**: Only click, ruamel.yaml, jinja2 specified

### Architecture Rules Alignment

- [x] **Separate concerns**: Parser → Extractor → Renderer pipeline with distinct modules (parser.py, extractor.py, renderer.py)
- [x] **Dataclasses, never dicts**: FR-003 explicitly requires typed dataclasses, never raw dicts
- [x] **Renderers consume dataclasses only**: FR-010 specifies renderer consumes PlaybookData/RoleData
- [x] **User-overridable templates**: FR-012 requires .anodyse/templates/ override with validation
- [x] **CLI delegates logic**: Spec requires "no business logic in CLI commands" (Stage 8)

### Output Rules Alignment

- [x] **UTF-8 Markdown**: FR-017 mandates UTF-8; FR-010 specifies Markdown output
- [x] **Slugified filenames**: FR-013 requires lowercase, hyphens, no special characters
- [x] **Mermaid optional**: FR-011 specifies --graph flag for diagrams
- [x] **Index always generated**: FR-014 mandates index.md listing all items
- [x] **Backup before overwrite**: FR-015 requires .bak files unless --no-backup

### Testing Requirements Alignment

- [x] **Unit tests**: Spec requires parser, extractor, renderer unit tests (Stage 9)
- [x] **Integration test**: Stage 9 requires full pipeline test with fixture
- [x] **CI Python 3.11 and 3.12**: Specified in Stage 0 (GitHub Actions workflow)
- [x] **Coverage ≥80%**: SC-010 explicitly requires this threshold

### Constitution Prohibitions Check

- [x] **No source file modification**: SC-009 validates read-only operation
- [x] **No assumption of undocumented intent**: FR-007 sets None for missing @description, does not infer
- [x] **No network at runtime**: Assumptions section confirms offline requirement
- [x] **No internal architecture exposure**: Spec defers credentials/hostnames to constitution; Out of Scope confirms no vault decryption

### Gate Result: ✅ PASS

All constitution principles, standards, rules, and prohibitions are satisfied by the spec. No violations requiring justification.

**Post-Phase 1 Re-Check**: ✅ PASS

After completing data model design, contracts definition, and quickstart guide:
- Data models use stdlib dataclasses (minimal dependencies maintained)
- All interfaces documented with stable contracts (CLI, templates, manifest)
- Architecture follows strict separation of concerns (parser → extractor → renderer → output)
- Testing requirements met: unit + integration tests planned, coverage ≥80%
- All design decisions align with constitution principles

No new violations introduced during Phase 1 design.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/[###-feature]/
├── plan.md              # This file (/speckit.plan command output)
├── research.md          # Phase 0 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── contracts/           # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)
└── tasks.md             # Phase 2 output (/speckit.tasks command - NOT created by /speckit.plan)
```

### Source Code (repository root)
<!--
  ACTION REQUIRED: Replace the placeholder tree below with the concrete layout
  for this feature. Delete unused options and expand the chosen structure with
  real paths (e.g., apps/admin, packages/something). The delivered plan must
  not include Option labels.
-->

```text
anodyse/                  # Main package
├── __init__.py
├── cli.py               # Click CLI entry point
├── discovery.py         # Directory scanner + manifest loader (FR-009)
├── parser.py            # YAML parsing (FR-003, FR-004)
├── extractor.py         # Annotation extraction (FR-005–FR-008)
├── renderer.py          # Jinja2 rendering (FR-010–FR-012)
├── output.py            # File writing + backup (FR-013–FR-017)
├── models.py            # PlaybookData, RoleData, TaskData dataclasses
├── exceptions.py        # ParseError, AnnotationWarning
└── templates/           # Default Jinja2 templates
    ├── playbook.md.j2
    ├── role.md.j2
    └── index.md.j2

tests/
├── unit/
│   ├── test_parser.py
│   ├── test_extractor.py
│   ├── test_renderer.py
│   └── test_discovery.py
├── integration/
│   └── test_pipeline.py  # Full parse-to-output test
└── fixtures/
    ├── playbook_annotated.yml
    ├── playbook_unannotated.yml
    ├── role_sample/
    │   ├── tasks/main.yml
    │   ├── defaults/main.yml
    │   ├── meta/main.yml
    │   └── vars/main.yml
    └── manifest_sample.yml  # .anodyse.yml example

pyproject.toml           # Dependencies + ruff config
.github/
└── workflows/
    └── ci.yml           # Lint + test on push/PR (Python 3.11, 3.12)
```

**Structure Decision**: Single Python package with clear separation of concerns (discovery → parse → extract → render → output). CLI delegates all business logic to core modules. Templates are user-overridable via .anodyse/templates/ at invocation directory.

---

## Phase 0: Research & Technical Decisions

**Status**: ✅ Complete

All technical decisions documented and justified. See [research.md](research.md) for detailed analysis of:
- Click vs Typer for CLI framework
- ruamel.yaml for comment-preserving YAML parsing
- Regex pattern matching for annotation extraction
- pathlib.Path.rglob() for directory discovery
- Custom slugification (no external dependency)
- Simple Mermaid sequence diagrams for MVP
- Render-test validation for user templates
- Include/exclude manifest structure

**Key Outcomes**:
- No external API dependencies
- Minimal dependency footprint (click, ruamel.yaml, jinja2 only)
- All NEEDS CLARIFICATION items resolved

---

## Phase 1: Design Artifacts

**Status**: ✅ Complete

All design artifacts generated and reviewed:

### Data Model: [data-model.md](data-model.md)

Defines 4 core dataclasses:
- **TaskData**: Individual task with optional task-level annotations
- **PlaybookData**: Complete playbook structure with extracted annotations
- **RoleData**: Complete role structure with annotated parameters only
- **IndexEntry**: Summary entry for index.md generation

All dataclasses use Python stdlib (no Pydantic), fully type-hinted, with clear validation rules.

### Contracts: [contracts/](contracts/)

Three stable contracts documented:

1. **[CLI Command Contract](contracts/cli-command.md)**
   - Command signature: `anodyse [OPTIONS] <target>`
   - Exit codes: 0 (success), 1 (error), 2 (warning)
   - Discovery modes: directory scan + manifest override
   - Guaranteed stable options and behavior

2. **[Template Variables Contract](contracts/template-variables.md)**
   - Guaranteed context variables for playbook/role/index templates
   - Validation strategy for user templates
   - Common Jinja2 patterns and examples

3. **[Manifest Schema Contract](contracts/manifest-schema.md)**
   - `.anodyse.yml` structure with include/exclude lists
   - Mutual exclusivity rules and validation
   - Path resolution and error handling

### Developer Guide: [quickstart.md](quickstart.md)

9-stage implementation guide with:
- Prerequisites and environment setup
- Stage-by-stage implementation (0–9)
- Test requirements for each stage
- Development workflow and troubleshooting
- Milestone checklist

**Estimated Implementation Time**: 3-5 days for experienced Python developer

---

## Implementation Stages Summary

| Stage | Module | Estimated Time | Key Deliverables |
|-------|--------|----------------|------------------|
| 0 | Scaffold | 30 min | pyproject.toml, package structure, CI |
| 1 | models.py | 1 hour | TaskData, PlaybookData, RoleData, IndexEntry |
| 2 | exceptions.py | 15 min | ParseError, AnnotationWarning, ManifestError |
| 3 | discovery.py | 2 hours | Directory scan + manifest loader |
| 4 | parser.py | 3 hours | YAML parsing with ruamel.yaml |
| 5 | extractor.py | 3 hours | Annotation extraction with regex |
| 6 | renderer.py | 4 hours | Jinja2 rendering + user template validation |
| 7 | output.py | 2 hours | File writing, slugification, backups |
| 8 | cli.py | 3 hours | Click CLI with all options |
| 9 | Tests | 4 hours | Unit + integration tests (≥80% coverage) |

**Total**: 22.75 hours (approximately 3 days)
